We care about the quality of our survey data. For us to get the most accurate measures, it is important that you provide thoughtful answers to each question in this survey. Please consider questions thoughtfully. 

Do not rush or 'straight-line'. We see responses. 

Do you commit to providing thoughtful answers to the questions in this survey?
attn1: Write the number corresponding to "twenty-five" here.
dv_llm_usage: Do you ever use artificial intelligence chatbots such as ChatGPT, Anthropic's Claude, Google Gemini, Grok, or an open source chatbot?
dv_use_moral:
Have you ever used a chatbot to help you think through what's right or wrong? Examples include:


- Asking for advice on an ethical dilemma you're facing (like whether to report a coworker's mistake or how to handle a friend's request) 
- Getting help deciding if something is morally acceptable or the "right thing to do"

- Discussing questions about fairness, honesty, or what you owe to others
dv_use_moral_feed: [OPTIONAL BUT HELPFUL] Is this question clear? Why or why not?
dv_use_moral_desc: [OPTIONAL BUT HELPFUL] Can you describe your usage? How did things work out?
dv_use_personal:
Have you ever used a chatbot for personal advice or to better understand yourself or your relationships? Examples include:


- Getting advice about relationships, dating, friendships, or family situations

- Asking for help understanding your own feelings, motivations, or personality

- Seeking guidance on personal decisions, life choices, or how to handle emotional situations
dv_use_personal_feed: [OPTIONAL BUT HELPFUL] Is this question clear? Why or why not?
dv_use_personal_desc: [OPTIONAL BUT HELPFUL] Can you describe your usage? How did things work out?
dv_use_con:
Have you ever used a chatbot to understand what is socially acceptable or expected? Examples include:


- Checking whether something you're planning to say or do follows social norms or expectations

- Asking what the appropriate thing to do or say in a social situation (like how to behave at a wedding, job interview, or dinner party) 
- Getting advice on social etiquette or customs (like tipping practices, dress codes, or greeting customs)
dv_use_con_feed: [OPTIONAL BUT HELPFUL] Is this question clear? Why or why not?
dv_use_con_desc: [OPTIONAL BUT HELPFUL] Can you describe your usage? How did things work out?

dv_use_personal_feed: Have you ever used ChatGPT as a therapist or coach or friend?
dv_use_personal_desc: I've used CGPT to explore feelings after a breakup and with losing community. It did a much better job of active listening than humans can; i didn't feel judged even if I didn't get any actionable items out of talking to it.

dv_use_con_feed: Yes. Have you ever asked it a social etiquette questions?
dv_use_con_desc: I asked if what the appropriate amount was to spend on a gift in the office Christmas exchange. They said 10-20 bucks but then people started going big with the last gift. So, I used ChatGPT to spitball how I would look if I did or didn't do it, since I am a tightwad and don't want to spend $50 when I could spend $20. ChatGPT did not give me an answer but gave me a space to realize that I could manage perception of me by giving the gift in a different way an saying I misunderstood or forgot the changed final gift rule so that my smaller gift was not showcased.
